list_items: What learning capabilities do humans possess, yet RL agents still missing? [1,2]
Knowledge-Acquirable: Develop unique policies by observing.
· Compositional: Combine policies from multiple sources to form a knowledge set.

img_path: output/Flexible Attention-Based Multi-Policy Fusion for Efficient Deep Reinforcement Learning_poster/visuals/tables/table_1.jpg
output/Flexible Attention-Based Multi-Policy Fusion for Efficient Deep Reinforcement Learning_poster/visuals/tables/table_2.jpg

list_items: Incremental: Arbitrarily remove outdated policies or add new ones, no need to relearn how to navigate the entire knowledge set from scratch.
· Sample-Efficient: Require fewer interactions.
Generalizable: Be able to apply previously learned policies to unseen tasks.
KGRL: An RL paradigm aiming to learn with external policies and achieve the five properties above.

text: KIAN: An actor that separates knowledge representations from their selection mechanism.

list_items: ·Inner Actor
· Knowledge Keys
·Query
